name: Ramen Taro
description: Authentic Japanese ramen restaurant in the heart of Tokyo, serving traditional Hakata-style tonkotsu ramen since 1985. Our 48-hour simmered broth and handmade noodles deliver an unforgettable dining experience in Higashiyamato.

seo.title: Ramen Taro | Authentic Hakata Ramen in Higashiyamato, Tokyo
seo.description: Experience authentic Hakata ramen at Ramen Taro since 1985. Our 48-hour tonkotsu broth and handmade noodles deliver the true taste of Fukuoka. Located in Higashiyamato, Tokyo. Open for lunch & dinner. Reservations recommended.

content.shortDescription: Traditional Japanese ramen featuring 48-hour simmered tonkotsu broth and handmade noodles in Higashiyamato, Tokyo.
content.mission: To bring the authentic taste of Fukuoka to Tokyo through time-honored recipes and Omotenashi hospitality—where every guest is family.

about.title: Our Culinary Heritage
about.content: Founded in 1985 by Master Chef Takeshi Yamamoto, Ramen Taro began as a small street stall in Fukuoka's famous Ramen Alley before bringing authentic Hakata-style ramen to Tokyo. Our signature 48-hour tonkotsu broth is simmered slowly using pork bones from local farms, achieving a rich, creamy collagen-rich texture that defines true Hakata ramen. Every morning, our third-generation noodle makers handcraft fresh noodles using traditional kansansui (alkaline water) technique, ensuring the perfect 'bite' or 'koshi' that complements our savory broths. Beyond the food, Ramen Taro embodies Omotenashi—the Japanese philosophy of wholehearted hospitality—where every guest is treated like family and every detail is anticipated before asked. Whether you're a regular or first-time visitor, we promise an unforgettable taste of Fukuoka in the heart of Tokyo.
additionalContent: Our broth is never rushed. Exactly 48 hours of simmering creates depth of flavor that shortcuts cannot replicate. We start with 100kg of pork bones each morning, reducing it to a concentrated essence that's both rich and clean.

Noodles are made fresh daily in-house using a proprietary blend of wheat flours imported from Kyushu. The alkaline mineral water gives our noodles their signature springy texture and slight yellow hue.
We source ingredients with purpose: Berkshire pork for chashu, organic eggs from free-range chickens, and seasonal vegetables from the Tsukiji Market. Quality is non-negotiable.

founder.name: Takeshi Yamamoto
founder.bio: With over 40 years of experience, Master Chef Takeshi Yamamoto is a third-generation ramen artisan. Trained in Fukuoka's Ramen Alley under his father and grandfather, Takeshi brought authentic Hakata-style ramen to Tokyo in 1985. He has been awarded 'Best Ramen Chef' by the Tokyo Ramen Association three times (2018, 2020, 2023) and is a certified Ramen Artisan by the Fukuoka Ramen Association.
qualifications: Master of Traditional Noodle Making (1978-1985 apprenticeship)

about.mission: To bring the authentic taste of Fukuoka to every corner of Tokyo, preserving traditional techniques while embracing innovation for the future of Japanese cuisine.
about.philosophy: Omotenashi - the Japanese art of wholehearted hospitality that anticipates and fulfills every guest's needs before they even ask. Every bowl of ramen is crafted with the same care we would give our own family.
about.heritage: 三代続けての製法 (Three-generation traditional method)

team.name: Takeshi Yamamoto
team.bio: With over 40 years of experience, Takeshi is the heart of our kitchen and the guardian of our 48-hour broth tradition. Third-generation ramen artisan from Fukuoka.

team.name: Hanako Sato
team.bio: Hanako ensures every guest feels at home with her warm Omotenashi hospitality and seamless service. She manages daily operations with precision and care.

team.name: Kenji Tanaka
team.bio: Kenji's passion for fresh ingredients and modern techniques brings innovation to our classic menu while respecting tradition.

openingHours.notes: Regular hours except New Year's period

holidayNotes: Open 7 days a week. Closed only on New Year's Eve (Dec 31) and New Year's Day (Jan 1). May operate reduced hours on national holidays—please call ahead.

menu.name: Tonkotsu Ramen
menu.description: Rich pork bone broth simmered for 48 hours, thin noodles, chashu pork, soft-boiled egg, nori seaweed, and green onions

menu.name: Spicy Miso Ramen
menu.description: Miso-based broth with spicy ground pork, sweet corn, butter pat, bean sprouts, and garlic

menu.name: Vegetarian Miso Ramen
menu.description: Creamy miso broth with seasonal vegetables, deep-fried tofu, seaweed, and bamboo shoots

menu.name: Shoyu Ramen
menu.description: Classic soy sauce-based clear broth with bamboo shoots, nori, chashu, and boiled egg

menu.name: Truffle Ramen (Chef's Special)
menu.description: Specialty ramen infused with black truffle oil, topped with truffle shavings, chashu, and organic egg

menu.name: Gyoza (6pcs)
menu.description: Pan-fried pork and vegetable dumplings with crispy bottom and juicy filling. Served with house-made dipping sauce

menu.name: Chili Shrimp (Ebi Chili)
menu.description: Crispy fried shrimp in spicy sweet chili sauce

menu.name: Matcha Latte
menu.description: Creamy green tea latte, hot or iced

menu.name: Mochi Ice Cream
menu.description: Assorted flavors: strawberry, green tea, mango (2pcs)

menu.name: Matcha Cheesecake
menu.description: Rich green tea flavored cheesecake with graham cracker crust

videos.title: Making Authentic Tonkotsu Broth - Behind the Scenes at Ramen Taro
videos.description: Master Chef Takeshi Yamamoto explains our 48-hour broth preparation process and the secrets to authentic Hakata ramen.

individual.author: Mike T.
individual.reviewBody: Best ramen I've ever had outside of Fukuoka! The broth is incredibly rich and flavorful. Will definitely return on my next Tokyo trip.

individual.author: Sarah L.
individual.reviewBody: The Omotenashi service is genuine. Chef's special truffle ramen is worth every yen. A hidden gem in Higashiyamato.

privateDining.description: Private tatami room for 8-12 guests, ideal for business dinners and celebrations
